title: Bulk report - Week 30
summary:
Capesize
After a slow beginning to the week, the Capesize market experienced a surge on Wednesday with a rise of over $2,500 in a day on its five timecharter routes average. The week closed at $15,180 with +$3,222 week-on-week. Both fronthaul and trans-Atlantic business were particularly active whilst tonnage in the Atlantic tightened.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: After a slow beginning to the week, the Capesize market experienced a surge on Wednesday with a rise of over $2,500 in a day on its five timecharter routes average. The week closed at $15,180 with +$3,222 week-on-week.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: It was an eventful week with the Panamax market finally finding some life. The week began slowly, but eventually sparked and was duly accompanied by an FFA drive/some Cape splits in parts only to flatten out as the week ended.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: Another rather unexciting week for the sector, certainly from the Atlantic, which saw further drops with limited fresh impetus across most areas with the ongoing Summertime slow down. The US Gulf was described as positional whilst the South Atlantic had positional opportunities for owners with prompt vessels, although it did remain fairly uneventful.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: Limited enquiry in the South Atlantic has led to ever growing tonnage availability. A 32,000-dwt was fixed basis delivery Recalada for a trip to the US East Coast with an intended cargo of sugar and duration of about 50 days at $9,000.
